tests.minimum_expectation: at least one meaningful MCP test or explicit skip reason

target_files.legacy-to-mcp9/input-documents/petclinic: new REST client interface with @RegisterRestClient(configKey="petclinic") exposing listPets, getPet, getOwnersPet GET endpoints
new typed DTO for Pet schema (id, name, birthDate, type, ownerId, visits)
new typed DTO for PetType schema (id, name)
new MCP server class wrapping REST client calls as @Tool methods using ToolResponse + TextContent
add port 8888, CORS enabled, and petclinic rest-client URL pointing to localhost:9966
add MCP sidecar container to existing Deployment alongside petclinic container, exposing port 8888

tech_debt.Visit DTO not modeled: visits field on Pet is List<Visit> but Visit schema was not provided; use raw List<Object> or a minimal Visit stub until schema is confirmed — risk of deserialization failure if Visit fields are non-trivial
tech_debt.No TLS on sidecar port: sidecar listens on plain HTTP 8888 inside the pod; acceptable for in-cluster loopback but should be reviewed if network policy is tightened
tech_debt.application.properties currently empty: all required properties must be added from scratch; no existing values to preserve, but any future properties added by other means will not be detected by this plan

modernization_steps.task: Create PetType DTO with fields id (Integer) and name (String, required, max 80)
Create Pet DTO with fields id (Integer, read-only), name (String, max 30), birthDate (LocalDate, required), type (PetType, required), ownerId (Integer, read-only), visits (List<Object>, read-only)

Create PetclinicRestClient interface annotated with @RegisterRestClient(configKey="petclinic") and @Path; declare listPets(), getPet(@PathParam("petId") int petId), getOwnersPet(@PathParam("ownerId") int ownerId, @PathParam("petId") int petId) — all GET, no throws

Create PetclinicMcpServer @ApplicationScoped class; inject PetclinicRestClient with @Inject @RestClient and ObjectMapper with @Inject; implement three @Tool methods (listPets, getPet, getOwnersPet) each returning ToolResponse wrapping TextContent — no throws declarations, catch exceptions internally

Add MCP sidecar container to the Deployment spec in petclinic-kubernetes.yaml alongside the existing petclinic container; sidecar image should be the built Quarkus MCP image, containerPort 8888; no new Service or Route required

Write at least one Quarkus test for PetclinicMcpServer using @QuarkusTest and WireMock (or @InjectMock) to verify a @Tool method returns a non-empty ToolResponse; alternatively document explicit skip reason in a @Disabled annotation

evaluation_criteria: @Inject and @RestClient are both present on the PetclinicRestClient field inside PetclinicMcpServer
configKey value 'petclinic' matches the property key quarkus.rest-client.petclinic.url in application.properties
no throws clause on any @Tool-annotated method in PetclinicMcpServer
no new ObjectMapper() instantiation anywhere in the MCP server or REST client code
at least one meaningful MCP test exists in the test sources or an explicit skip reason is documented
sidecar container in petclinic-kubernetes.yaml exposes containerPort 8888
legacy Deployment container (petclinic) is not modified
PetclinicRestClient uses @RegisterRestClient(configKey="petclinic") with no hardcoded baseUri
Pet and PetType are typed DTOs, not raw Map or JsonNode
quarkus.http.port=8888 and quarkus.http.cors.enabled=true are both present in application.properties
